sources_audited.note: The BLS matrix reports the stated combined private NAICS 611400 population, 85.5 thousand jobs in 2024, 94.0 thousand in 2034, 9.9% growth, and the cited occupational shares, including 29.2% business and financial operations and 14.5% training and development specialists.

The BLS occupation page supports the stated needs-assessment, content-development, delivery, evaluation, and administrative duties; people-intensive and self-guided or online modalities; and 11% projected 2024-2034 employment growth.

The Census page supports the 611430 definition, direct-individual and employer channels, customization, and client-site delivery. It does not establish repeat revenue, fixed-fee or retainer contracting, eligibility rates, or savings retention, so its support for e and q is directional only.
The BLS table reports management employment of 13.6076 million in 2024 and 14.4410 million in 2034, 6.1% growth, and 1.1479 million average annual openings, matching the registered claim.

The Anthropic page reports 14.4 average years of education for Claude-covered tasks versus 13.2 economy-wide and explicitly warns that observed Claude.ai tasks may not map to real-world change. It is not 611430-specific and does not measure the record's a or rho values.

caveats: S3 establishes industry scope and customization options but does not evidence recurring employer revenue, fixed-fee or retainer pricing, or savings retention; e and q remain honest ESTIMATEs rather than source-measured inputs.
S5 is economy-wide Claude-use evidence rather than 611430-specific technical exposure or implementation evidence; it supports direction only, as the a bridge and rho caveats acknowledge.

The d5 base of 1.08 is somewhat stronger than a simple five-year conversion of the cited 6.1%-11% ten-year employment projections and also relies on qualitative demand drivers; a reasonable correction would not change the PRIORITY tier or VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action.

The lens transparently narrows a NAICS category that includes direct-to-individual short-duration seminars to repeat employer-funded providers; this coherent but favorable selection makes e, q, and o particularly dependent on firm-level validation.

The a and o bases are qualitative mappings from combined-industry and cross-employer evidence rather than 611430-weighted measurements; their PROXY labels and ranges are appropriate, but the point estimates should not be treated as observed.
summary: Mechanics pass and the five primitive-cited sources are reachable. The record is useful and internally coherent, with honest PROXY and ESTIMATE treatment, explicit bridges, and conclusions that follow from the evidence. Census and Anthropic evidence supports only part of the eligibility, capture, exposure, and implementation logic, and the favorable repeat-employer lens requires firm-level validation, but these limitations do not provide a correction that would change the base PRIORITY tier or the MODEL_CONDITIONED action of VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS.
